feed.title: P3 Systems Biology Lab @ The University of Virginia

entry.title: P3SysBioLab Student Poster Award
entry.content:
Beckett Lisle presented a poster at the Virginia Drug Discovery Consortium (VaDDC) Symposium 2026, Ten Years of Drug Discovery and Development in Virginia, held June 8–9, 2026, at The Inn at Virginia Tech and Skelton Conference Center in Blacksburg, Virginia. In recognition of his research presentation, he was awarded Second Place in the Student Poster Competition and received a $250 cash prize.

Conference details: Virginia Drug Discovery Consortium (VaDDC) Symposium 2026
entry.summary: Beckett Lisle presented a poster at the Virginia Drug Discovery Consortium (VaDDC) Symposium 2026, Ten Years of Drug Discovery and Development in Virginia, held June 8–9, 2026, at The Inn at Virginia Tech and Skelton Conference Center in Blacksburg, Virginia. In recognition of his research presentation, he was awarded Second Place in the Student Poster Competition and received a $250 cash prize.

entry.title: P3SysBioLab Poster Presentation
entry.content:
Beckett Lisle presented a poster at the Virginia Drug Discovery Consortium (VaDDC) Symposium 2026, Ten Years of Drug Discovery and Development in Virginia, held June 8–9, 2026, at The Inn at Virginia Tech and Skelton Conference Center in Blacksburg, Virginia.

The symposium brought together academic researchers, industry scientists, and trainees from across Virginia to discuss recent advances in drug discovery and development. The poster session provided an opportunity for participants to showcase ongoing research and engage with experts from universities and industry throughout the Commonwealth.

Conference details: Virginia Drug Discovery Consortium (VaDDC) Symposium 2026
entry.summary: Beckett Lisle presented a poster at the Virginia Drug Discovery Consortium (VaDDC) Symposium 2026, Ten Years of Drug Discovery and Development in Virginia, held June 8–9, 2026, at The Inn at Virginia Tech and Skelton Conference Center in Blacksburg, Virginia.

entry.title: P3SysBioLab is NIH/NCI funded
entry.content: We received Notice of Award for NIH/NCI Career Transition Award (K22) to continue our work on AML risk classification using biochemical lipids and potential therapeutic opportunities.
entry.summary: We received Notice of Award for NIH/NCI Career Transition Award (K22) to continue our work on AML risk classification using biochemical lipids and potential therapeutic opportunities.

entry.title: Beckett Lisle Oral Presentation
entry.content: Beckett Lisle presented his research at the 2024 UVA Undergraduate Research Symposium. Congratulations, Beckett!
entry.summary: Beckett Lisle presented his research at the 2024 UVA Undergraduate Research Symposium. Congratulations, Beckett!

entry.title: Summer Research Grants for Vicente
entry.content: P3SysBioLab undergraduate, Vicente Carvajal Villegas is awarded a prestigious Harrison Undergraduate Research Fellowship for his project, “Relationship between sphingolipid and STAT5 signaling in AML,” for 2026 Summer. He will be working on understanding how two sphingolipid subtypes in AML have distinct signaling through JAK/STAT pathway. Congratulations, Vicente! We look forward to seeing your continued success.
entry.summary: P3SysBioLab undergraduate, Vicente Carvajal Villegas is awarded a prestigious Harrison Undergraduate Research Fellowship for his project, “Relationship between sphingolipid and STAT5 signaling in AML,” for 2026 Summer. He will be working on understanding how two sphingolipid subtypes in AML have distinct signaling through JAK/STAT pathway. Congratulations, Vicente! We look forward to seeing your continued success.

entry.title: P3SysBioLab Poster Presentation
entry.content: Beckett Lisle presented his poster at ACS STEM Research Poster Session organized by the Department of Chemistry at the University of Virginia. The poster session brought together both graduate and undergraduate students from different departments at UVA.
entry.summary: Beckett Lisle presented his poster at ACS STEM Research Poster Session organized by the Department of Chemistry at the University of Virginia. The poster session brought together both graduate and undergraduate students from different departments at UVA.

entry.title: P3SysBioLab Flask Talk
entry.content: We presented a flask talk in Cancer Research and Data Science Symposium at the University of Virginia. The symposium brought together researchers from UVA Cancer Center and School of Data Science.
entry.summary: We presented a flask talk in Cancer Research and Data Science Symposium at the University of Virginia. The symposium brought together researchers from UVA Cancer Center and School of Data Science.

entry.title: Grant awarded
entry.content: Our collaborative project titled “NPM1 Mutation Specific Therapeutic Vulnerabilities in Acute Myeloid Leukemia” has been selected as an awardee for Hematologic Malignancies Translational Research Team (HM-TRT) Pilot Funding FY2026 ($25,000). This project is a collaboration between Dr. Francine Garrett-Bakelman, Dr. Subhash Prajapati and P3SysBioLab. Our undergraduate, Beckett Lisle contributed to the proposal and will work on this project. Congratulations Team!
entry.summary: Our collaborative project titled “NPM1 Mutation Specific Therapeutic Vulnerabilities in Acute Myeloid Leukemia” has been selected as an awardee for Hematologic Malignancies Translational Research Team (HM-TRT) Pilot Funding FY2026 ($25,000). This project is a collaboration between Dr. Francine Garrett-Bakelman, Dr. Subhash Prajapati and P3SysBioLab. Our undergraduate, Beckett Lisle contributed to the proposal and will work on this project. Congratulations Team!

entry.title: Media Feature
entry.content:
Our research identifying high-risk AML patients got featured on local news.

More details here:
UVA Researchers’ Blood Cancer Discovery Identifies High-Risk Patients and Could Improve Outcomes
entry.summary: Our research identifying high-risk AML patients got featured on local news.

entry.title: NCI P01 Awarded
entry.content:
Our collaborative team at UVA has been awarded a five-year NCI P01 grant to lead a national study seeking new treatments for Acute Myeloid Leukemia.

More details here:
UVA Research Team Awarded $13M NCI P01 for AML Study
entry.summary: Our collaborative team at UVA has been awarded a five-year NCI P01 grant to lead a national study seeking new treatments for Acute Myeloid Leukemia.
